# UpdateBar Agent Guide

UpdateBar is a CLI for tracking and updating user-approved recipes. It does not
generate recipes or commands. External agents may write recipe JSON; UpdateBar
validates it, stores it untrusted by default, and runs only user-approved command
fingerprints.

Use this workflow:

```bash
updatebar schema --json
updatebar guide agent
updatebar guide recipe
updatebar template recipe --kind npm --id my-tool --source my-tool > recipe.json
updatebar template manifest --kind npm --id my-tool --source my-tool > manifest.json
updatebar validate recipe.json --json --explain
updatebar add --from recipe.json --dry-run --json
updatebar add --from recipe.json --json
updatebar approvals my-tool --json
```

Do not approve commands silently. Show `check.cmd`, `latest.cmd`, and `update.cmd`
to the user, then call `updatebar approve <id> --field <field> --json` only after
explicit confirmation.

Security facts:

- imported and templated recipes are untrusted by default
- `status --json` is read-only and does not run shell/network
- approved recipe commands are not sandboxed
- child processes receive an allowlisted environment only
- `version_parse.regex` is supported; `version_parse.jq` is rejected
- background checks are opt-in via `updatebar background install --yes`
